steps.description: Update the existing core TipTap editor implementation to fully satisfy the Ship collaborative editing requirements: confirm/create the Y.Doc fragment named 'default', wire y-websocket to ws://localhost:3000/collaboration/{docType}:{docId}, keep y-indexeddb offline persistence, add cache-clear (message type 3) IndexedDB reset handling before sync, and handle WebSocket close codes 4100/4101/4403 with redirect/reconnect/error behaviors without breaking current Editor exports.

Adjust the unified editor wrapper so it passes the correct document type/doc id collaboration inputs, preserves lazy loading of the shared Editor component, and exposes any new callbacks/state needed for collaborative redirect/reconnect/error handling on /documents/:id.

Revise the custom mention extension and its rendering helpers so @ suggestions come from /api/search/mentions, mentions render as inline chips linking to people/documents, and any node-view / suggestion logic remains compatible with the updated collaborative editor flow.

Revise the image upload extension and image node rendering so drag/drop and paste uploads post to /api/files, insert image nodes into TipTap, and render as resizable images compatible with the collaborative document schema.
Revise the file attachment extension so non-image file uploads go through /api/files and render as attachment/download links in the shared editor experience.

Revise the slash command extension/menu so / commands expose the requested block actions (heading, bullet list, numbered list, task list, code block, table, image, divider) and remain integrated with the shared editor capabilities.
Revise the custom hypothesis block extension/component so it acts as the dedicated embedded plan/hypothesis block expected by the editor and stays compatible with server-side extraction behavior.
Update the backlinks panel implementation so it fetches backlinks for the current document from /api/documents/:id/backlinks and renders the list below the editor in the unified document experience.
Update the inline comment display stack so highlighted comment marks render popover/thread UI with replies and resolve support, matching the requested inline comment behavior inside the editor.

Revise the properties sidebar implementation used by the editor portal so it renders the requested metadata (type, issue status, priority, assignee via PersonCombobox, belongs_to chips, visibility, created/updated timestamps) and updates documents through TanStack Query-backed PATCH mutations.

Wire the document page route /documents/:id to fetch the document, initialize/render the collaborative editor and portal sidebar, pass mutation handlers, and react properly to document conversion/reconnect/access-revoked collaboration events.

tool_input.command: cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260327c && pwd && find . -maxdepth 3 -type f | sed 's#^./##' | sort | head -300

messages.content:
Implement the TipTap rich-text editor component with collaborative editing for Ship documents. This is the core editing experience used across all document types (wiki, issue, project, program, weekly plans, etc.).

Create `src/components/Editor.tsx` (or `UnifiedEditor.tsx`) that initializes a TipTap editor with StarterKit, plus these extensions: Collaboration (Yjs binding to a shared Y.Doc fragment named 'default'), CollaborationCursor (awareness with user name/color), Placeholder, Link, TaskList+TaskItem, Table+TableRow+TableCell+TableHeader, CodeBlockLowlight, Dropcursor. Connect to the WebSocket collaboration server at `ws://localhost:3000/collaboration/{docType}:{docId}` using y-websocket's WebsocketProvider. Use y-indexeddb for offline persistence (IndexedDB provider). Handle the cache-clear message (type 3) from the server by clearing IndexedDB before sync.

Create custom extensions: MentionExtension (trigger with @, fetch suggestions from /api/search/mentions, render as inline chip linking to the mentioned document/person), ImageUpload (drag-and-drop/paste images, upload to /api/files, insert as resizable image node), FileAttachment (upload non-image files, render as download link), SlashCommands (trigger with /, show a menu of block types: heading, bullet list, numbered list, task list, code block, table, image, divider), HypothesisBlock (custom node for embedding plan/hypothesis content that gets extracted server-side).

Create `src/components/editor/BacklinksPanel.tsx` that fetches backlinks for the current document from /api/documents/:id/backlinks and displays them as a list below the editor. Create `src/components/editor/CommentDisplay.tsx` for inline comments (highlight marked text, show comment thread in a popover, support replies and resolve).

Create a properties sidebar that renders next to the editor via React Portal into `#properties-portal`. The sidebar shows document metadata: type, status (for issues), priority, assignee (PersonCombobox), belongs_to associations (program/project/sprint chips), visibility toggle, created/updated timestamps. Changes in the sidebar call PATCH on the document API and update via TanStack Query mutation.

Wire the editor into the document page: when navigating to /documents/:id, fetch the document, initialize the Yjs provider, render the editor with the properties sidebar. Handle WebSocket close codes: 4100 (document converted, redirect to new doc), 4101 (content updated via API, reconnect), 4403 (access revoked, show error).

Verify: open a document, type text, confirm it persists (reload and content appears), open the same document in two tabs, type in one, confirm text appears in the other within 1 second, confirm cursor presence shows the other user's name and color.

## Summary
Outcome: FAILED
Instruction: Implement the TipTap rich-text editor component with collaborative editing for Ship documents. This is the core editing experience used across all document types (wiki, issue, project, program, weekly plans, etc.).

Create `src/components/Editor.tsx` (or `UnifiedEditor.tsx`) that initializes a TipTap editor with StarterKit, plus these extensions: Collaboration (Yjs binding to a shared Y.Doc fragment named 'default'), CollaborationCursor (awareness with user name/color), Placeholder, Link, TaskList+TaskItem, Table+TableRow+TableCell+TableHeader, CodeBlockLowlight, Dropcursor. Connect to the WebSocket collaboration server at `ws://localhost:3000/collaboration/{docType}:{docId}` using y-websocket's WebsocketProvider. Use y-indexeddb for offline persistence (IndexedDB provider). Handle the cache-clear message (type 3) from the server by clearing IndexedDB before sync.

Create custom extensions: MentionExtension (trigger with @, fetch suggestions from /api/search/mentions, render as inline chip linking to the mentioned document/person), ImageUpload (drag-and-drop/paste images, upload to /api/files, insert as resizable image node), FileAttachment (upload non-image files, render as download link), SlashCommands (trigger with /, show a menu of block types: heading, bullet list, numbered list, task list, code block, table, image, divider), HypothesisBlock (custom node for embedding plan/hypothesis content that gets extracted server-side).

Create `src/components/editor/BacklinksPanel.tsx` that fetches backlinks for the current document from /api/documents/:id/backlinks and displays them as a list below the editor. Create `src/components/editor/CommentDisplay.tsx` for inline comments (highlight marked text, show comment thread in a popover, support replies and resolve).

Create a properties sidebar that renders next to the editor via React Portal into `#properties-portal`. The sidebar shows document metadata: type, status (for issues), priority, assignee (PersonCombobox), belongs_to associations (program/project/sprint chips), visibility toggle, created/updated timestamps. Changes in the sidebar call PATCH on the document API and update via TanStack Query mutation.

Wire the editor into the document page: when navigating to /documents/:id, fetch the document, initialize the Yjs provider, render the editor with the properties sidebar. Handle WebSocket close codes: 4100 (document converted, redirect to new doc), 4101 (content updated via API, reconnect), 4403 (access revoked, show error).

Verify: open a document, type text, confirm it persists (reload and content appears), open the same document in two tabs, type in one, confirm text appears in the other within 1 second, confirm cursor presence shows the other user's name and color.
